data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Monday: VZ, LLY, DPZ, META
data.description:
Stock index futures were lower on Monday as investors looked forward to Big Tech earnings slated to come in this week and continued to monitor developments in the U.S.-Iran conflict.

Here are the four stocks to watch on the day:

Meta Platforms (META) was flat before the opening bell after reports that China blocked its planned $2B acquisition of AI startup Manus. The National Development and Reform Commission ordered the deal’s cancellation, citing compliance with laws and regulations in a brief notice. The decision, reported by Bloomberg, provided no further details.

Eli Lilly (LLY) edged up 0.1% in early action after agreeing to acquire Ajax Therapeutics for up to $2.3B in cash. The deal includes upfront and milestone-based payments tied to clinical and regulatory progress. The acquisition expands Lilly’s presence in blood cancer therapies.

Verizon Communications (VZ) rose 3.9% ahead of the open after reporting Q1 non-GAAP EPS of $1.28, beating estimates by $0.07. Revenue of $34.4B increased year over year but missed expectations by $490M. The company pointed to disciplined promotional spending and moderated upgrade activity affecting equipment sales.

Domino's Pizza (DPZ) dropped 4% in early trading ahead of the open after reporting quarterly revenue of $1.15B, missing estimates by $10M. Same-store sales growth of 1.5% in U.S. company-owned stores and 0.8% in franchised locations also fell short of forecasts. The company cited continued order growth and market share gains in the U.S. despite the miss.

data.title: First BanCorp. upgraded to Strong Buy at Raymond James after Q1 results
data.description:
First BanCorp. (FBP) was upgraded to Strong Buy from Outperform at Raymond James after first-quarter financial results.

The bank delivered a Q1 non-GAAP EPS of $0.57, which beats expectations by $0.06, and revenue of $258.65M (+4.2% Y/Y), which was in line.

"FBP reported solid core results in 1Q26, marked by strong NIM expansion, well-controlled expenses, modest improvements in asset quality, and healthy capital returns," said analyst Steve Moss.

"While softer consumer demand tempered loan growth, originations grew 6% y/y and business activity in Puerto Rico remains robust due to reconstruction, onshoring, and expanded U.S. military operations," added Moss in a research note.

"Profitability is strong (1.9% ROA, 18.7% ROTCE) and we expect earnings growth to continue from loan growth, asset/liability repricing, well-controlled expenses, and share repurchases," said the research note.

"We believe the valuation will improve over time with continued execution and share repurchases," said the note.

The price target was raised to $27.00 from $26.00. FBP had closed at $23.41 on Friday.

The average Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating on the stock stands at Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system grades FBP as Hold.

data.title: Bitmine Immersion Technologies nears 5% ETH supply; holdings hit $13.3B
data.description:
Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) announced on Monday that its total crypto, cash, and “moonshot” investments are now worth $13.3B, as the company recently uplisted to the NYSE.

The company holds ~5.08M Ethereum (ETH-USD) (~$12B at $2.37K/ETH) and 200 Bitcoin (BTC-USD), along with $200M in Beast Industries and $91M in Eightco Holdings, plus $940M in cash. Its ETH stash alone equals 4.21% of the total supply.

Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) added ~102K ETH in just one week, showing aggressive accumulation. Chairman Thomas Lee said the company is now close to its goal of owning 5% of all ETH.

Out of total holdings, ~3.7M ETH (~$8.8B) is already staked, generating ~$264M yearly income, with the potential to reach ~$363M annually once fully deployed.

The firm is now the #1 Ethereum treasury globally and overall #2 in crypto holdings, behind Strategy (MSTR), which holds a much larger Bitcoin (BTC-USD) reserve.

The stock also sees strong activity, with ~$845M average daily trading volume, ranking #129 in the U.S.—just behind Nike (NKE) and ahead of Comfort Systems USA (FIX).

The stock (BMNR) is trading around ~$22.10 pre-market, while Ethereum (ETH-USD) is slightly down near ~$2.31K at the time of writing.

data.title: Datavault AI signs term sheet for $120M funding from Scilex
data.description:
• Datavault AI (NASDAQ: DVLT) on Monday said it has entered a binding term sheet with Scilex Holding Company for a $120 million cash contribution to fund its quantum-ready GPU edge network expansion.
  • Under the agreement, Scilex will receive a share of future revenues generated by Datavault AI’s network, with structured revenue participation tiers capped at up to $1.2 billion in aggregate payments.
  • Datavault AI said the funding will support deployment of a 100-city “Quantum-Ready Edge Network” in the United States, including infrastructure build-out and working capital needs.
  • The company said it expects to combine the funding with other sources, including asset sales and receivable collections, targeting more than $200 million in total cash inflows without equity dilution.
  • Datavault AI and Scilex said they will work toward a definitive agreement, with initial deployments expected to begin in 2026, subject to closing and execution of final terms.
  • DVLT +2.78% premarket to $0.75.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Shell to buy Canada's ARC Resources in $16.4B cash and stock deal
data.description:
Shell  (SHEL) said Monday it ​agreed to acquire ​Canadian ​energy company ARC Resources (AETUF) in a deal valued ​at US$16.4B, ⁠including ​debt, in a deal it said will increase its exposure to the Motney shale basin in British Columbia and Alberta.

Arc's (AETUF) operations are located in the same region as Shell's (SHEL) existing Groundbirch asset in British Columbia and Gold Creek project in Alberta.

Under the deal terms, Shell (SHEL) said it will pay ​ARC (AETUF) shareholders C$8.20/share in cash and 0.40247 Shell (SHEL) shares for each ARC share, representing ~25% cash and 75% shares; based on Shell's latest closing share price, the deal equates to C$32.80/share, which represents a 20% premium to ARC's 30-day volume-weighted average price.

Shell (SHEL) will take on ~US$2.8B in net debt and leases, resulting in a total enterprise value of ~US$16.4B.

The company said the deal adds 370K boe/day of production, raising its production compound annual growth rate from 1% as previously outlined at its 2025 Capital Markets Day to 4%, and ​2B barrels of ​reserves, ⁠and supports its aim to sustain material liquids production of ~1.4M bbl/day towards 2030 and beyond.

Shell (SHEL) said it expects the deal to bring annualized synergies of ~$250M within a year of closing.

data.title: ACM Research expects to report Q1 revenue growth of 31% to 33%
data.description:
• ACM Research (ACMR) said on Monday that its preliminary unaudited revenue for the first quarter of 2026 is expected to be in the range of $225M to $230M (vs. estimates of $216.47M), which would represent year-to-year growth of 31% to 33%.
  • Preliminary total shipments for the first quarter of 2026 are expected to be in the range of $233M to $238M, translating to a year-to-year growth of 49% to 52%.  
  • It also reaffirmed thefull-year 2026 revenue outlook in the range of $1.08B to $1.175B, representing year-to-year growth of 20% to 30%.
  • ACM will discuss its full financial results on Thursday, May 7, 2026, before markets open.
  • ACMR -0.25% premarket to $55.96.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ralph Lauren leads all large-cap consumer discretionary stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap consumer discretionary stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans multiple sub-sectors, including Apparel, Accessories and Luxury Goods, Restaurants, Home Furnishings, Automotive Retail, Hotels, Resorts and Cruise Lines, Broadline Retail, Casinos and Gaming, and Automobile Manufacturers.

Ralph Lauren (RL) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 147.79% year-over-year, driven by continued investment in retail store expansion and brand elevation, with total capital expenditures rising to $216 million in Fiscal 2025 from $165 million in the prior year.

DoorDash (DASH) and Somnigroup International (SGI) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 147.12% and 71.53% respectively, while Carvana (CVNA) and Royal Caribbean Cruises (RCL) round out the top five at 61.54% and 60.01%.

Amazon (AMZN) and Wynn Resorts (WYNN) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 58.82% and 57.27%, while DraftKings (DKNG) and Rivian Automotive (RIVN) follow at 50.86% and 49.87%.

CAVA Group (CAVA) rounds out the list at 46.77%, reflecting the Mediterranean fast-casual chain's ongoing restaurant expansion, with 72 net new locations opened in fiscal 2025.

Somnigroup International (SGI) is the only stock on the list with a Sell rating at 2.02, while the remaining names all carry Hold ratings ranging from 2.64 to 3.44 under Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings, making this one of the few cross-sector CapEx growth lists without a single Buy or Strong Buy recommendation.

Here is the list:

  1. Ralph Lauren Corporation (RL), CapEx growth YoY: 147.79%

  2. DoorDash, Inc. (DASH), CapEx growth YoY: 147.12%

  3. Somnigroup International Inc. (SGI), CapEx growth YoY: 71.53%

  4. Carvana Co. (CVNA), CapEx growth YoY: 61.54%

  5. Royal Caribbean Cruises Ltd. (RCL), CapEx growth YoY: 60.01%

  6. Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN), CapEx growth YoY: 58.82%

  7. Wynn Resorts, Limited (WYNN), CapEx growth YoY: 57.27%

  8. DraftKings Inc. (DKNG), CapEx growth YoY: 50.86%

  9. Rivian Automotive, Inc. (RIVN), CapEx growth YoY: 49.87%

  10. CAVA Group, Inc. (CAVA), CapEx growth YoY: 46.77%

Consumer Discretionary ETFs: (XLY), (VCR), (FXD), (FDIS), (RSPD), and (RXI)

data.title: GameSquare reports record Q1 bookings for experiential marketing unit
data.description:
• GameSquare Holdings (NASDAQ:GAME) on Monday said its GameSquare Experiences 'GSX' division generated over $10 million in bookings in the first quarter of 2026, marking a record performance.
  • The company said growth was driven by demand from video game publishers and global brands, alongside increased adoption of its digital-to-physical marketing campaigns.
  • GameSquare said GSX has expanded its recurring client base more than threefold since its launch in 2024 and increased revenue about 14 times over the same period.
  • The division focuses on experiential and creator-led marketing campaigns that integrate digital content with real-world activations.
  • GAME +6.86% premarket to $0.638.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Adobe downgraded at Mizuho amid 'weakened' conviction
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 1.5% in premarket trading on Monday after Mizuho Securities downgraded the Photoshop maker, citing “weakened” conviction.

“Our conviction has weakened as we see intensifying competition in the prosumer/SMB segments threatening ADBE's long-term terminal value, along with no clear catalyst for the stock, and a risk of margin erosion,” analyst Gregg Moskowitz wrote in a note to clients. 

Moskowitz continued: “While we believe ADBE is making some more progress monetizing its Generative AI innovations, we also believe its organic revenue/ARR CAGR over the next 2-3 years is high-single-digits at best, and we much prefer owning assets at very reasonable (if not depressed) valuations that possess meaningful potential for upward estimate revisions. We see a generally balanced risk/reward profile on ADBE from here, and are thus downgrading to Neutral from Outperform and lowering our PT to $270 from $315.”

Delving deeper, Moskowitz said that he has become "incrementally more concerned"as Adobe's consumer and small-and-medium-sized business tiers face greater competition from AI-native platforms and low-cost design tools. And with the barriers to entry falling, lower-end users may bypass Adobe's Creative Cloud altogether, Moskowitz posited. 

Additionally, Moskowitz said he does not see a catalyst that can re-rate the stock over the next 6 to 12 months, as revenue guidance implies only 9.5% year-over-year growth, below management's aspiration for double-digit growth. There are only concerns that margins could decline, due to the aforementioned competitive threats and rising inference costs for Firefly.
